scope: TorchTitan DeepSeek-V4 shared-KV sparse-MLA TileLang dKV e2e stage profile

step_1.read: compile and warmup polluted; do not use for bottleneck ranking

single_boundary_reference.read: The isolated actual-LI boundary profile says TileLang's selected-limit dKV body is useful, but the materialized bridge around it is still about 21.5 ms before compressor/LI integration.

read: TileLang is promising, but the e2e run shows the win is currently trapped inside a larger backward bridge. At the TorchTitan step boundary, backward is still the dominant wall at 11.4 s of a 16.2 s step. Inside shared-KV sparse attention backward, the TileLang compressed-dKV region is the largest per-call stage, but the surrounding materialized bridge is also large: route planning, selected-KV gather, QK/probability/grad-logit rebuild, dQ, local dKV math, and local scatter add up to roughly another 13 ms per call. That explains why a 6-8 ms TileLang dKV kernel did not move TFLOPs much by itself.
bottleneck_ranking: Whole step: backward dominates, not forward or optimizer.
Sparse-MLA backward call: compressed TileLang dKV body is still the largest single stage.
Bridge overhead around TileLang is the next attention-local wall: materialized selected-KV, probability/grad-logit slabs, separate QK/PV/dQ stages, and route planning.
Therefore the next TileLang work should be a fused/adjacent sparse-MLA backward boundary, not only a faster final dKV store kernel.
next_profile_actions: Run a no-profile or minimal-profile 4x/8x e2e A/B with TileLang on/off after the first fused bridge change, because this profile intentionally synchronizes many stages.
Add one attention-layer call counter keyed by layer/compress_ratio so per-call TileLang costs can be mapped to layer 2 versus layer 3 instead of only aggregate stage labels.
Profile a fused TileLang boundary candidate that consumes q, dO, compressed K/V, top_indices, and row stats directly, avoiding selected-KV/probs/grad_logits HBM slabs.
Keep the full-step additive timer enabled for 2-step diagnostics only; use profiler-off runs for promoted TFLOPs.

negative_control.read: This earlier 8x run was misconfigured: it did not force shared_kv backward mode or the shared-KV kernel module, so it is retained only as negative evidence and not used for TileLang bottleneck ranking.

commands: TARGET_HOST=do-sonle5-mi350-gpu REMOTE_ROOT=/scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527 RUN_ID=torchtitan_dsv4_sharedkv_tilelang_stage_profile_4x_s4096_gbs64_steps2_20260604 GPUS_PER_NODE=4 CANARY_VISIBLE_DEVICES=4,5,6,7 CANARY_EP=4 CANARY_GBS=64 CANARY_STEPS=2 CANARY_SPARSE_MLA_BACKWARD_MODE=shared_kv CANARY_SPARSE_MLA_KERNEL_MODULE=torchtitan.models.deepseek_v4.sparse_mla_shared_kv_triton_forward_backend CANARY_SHAREDKV_BACKWARD_MODE=explicit_tilelang_dkv CANARY_SHAREDKV_TILELANG_DKV_BACKEND=selected_limit_hsk_bf16_scaled_source CANARY_DSV4_PROFILE_TIMING_PATH=/scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527/outputs/torchtitan_dsv4_sharedkv_tilelang_stage_profile_4x_s4096_gbs64_steps2_20260604/profiling/profile_timing CANARY_DSV4_PROFILE_SHAREDKV_BACKWARD=true CANARY_DSV4_ADDITIVE_TIMING_PATH=/scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527/outputs/torchtitan_dsv4_sharedkv_tilelang_stage_profile_4x_s4096_gbs64_steps2_20260604/profiling/additive_timing.jsonl experiments/2026-05-27-deepseek-v4-amd-pretrain/launchers/torchtitan_direct_deepseek_v4_4x8xmi350_canary.sh run
